Title: Construction of a single-cell transcriptome atlas for Pogostemon embryoids reveals PcNAC048 as a cablindual regulator coordinating lateral root morphogenesis and patchouli alcohol biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Downstream sesquiterpene branch from the mevalonate-derived FPP pool
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Core component of patchouli essential oil with antiviral, antibacterial, and antitumor activities; used in perfumery, cosmetics, soaps, and medicinal applications
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in leaves; reduced upon PcNAC048 overexpression and increased upon PcNAC048 silencing
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf tissues analyzed in transient overexpression and VIGS assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient overexpression of PcNAC048; virus-induced gene silencing of PcNAC048
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR of PcPTS transcript coupled with GC-MS quantification of patchouli alcohol under PcNAC048 transient overexpression and VIGS backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PcPTS expression positively associates with patchouli alcohol accumulation and is repressed by PcNAC048
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcPTS is the downstream synthase gene directly associated with patchouli alcohol production. 2. PcNAC048 binds the PcPTS promoter and suppresses promoter activity. 3. PcPTS transcript decreases during PcNAC048 overexpression and increases during PcNAC048 silencing, matching changes in patchouli alcohol content.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A key biosynthetic target for engineering patchouli alcohol yield; likely beneficial to activate or protect from repressors such as PcNAC048
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The promoter fragment tested was 1865 bp (PcPTSPro); PcPTS was used as the immediate readout target of PcNAC048 repression in Y1H and dual-LUC assays.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAM/NAC superfamily; conserved NAC domain with subdomains A-E, with C and D implicated in DNA binding
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in dual-LUC effector and Arabidopsis overexpression constructs
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold (4 °C), salt (200 mM NaCl), drought (20% PEG6000); transient overexpression in P. cablin leaves; VIGS silencing in P. cablin leaves; heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis thaliana
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional repression of the terminal biosynthetic step of patchouli alcohol biosynthesis; coordination of secondary metabolism with lateral root morphogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: scRNA-seq/pseudotime and GRN prioritization; RT-qPCR expression profiling; subcellular localization; Arabidopsis overexpression; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assay; transient overexpression in P. cablin; VIGS in P. cablin; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Pogostemon cablin; Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Cold, salt, and drought stress; developmental differentiation trajectory toward epidermal cells
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds the PcPTS promoter and inhibits PcPTS transcription/promoter activity; predicted to regulate HMGR2, TPS11, and TPS7 in the GRN
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Core component of patchouli essential oil with antiviral, antibacterial, and antitumor activities; valuable for medicinal and fragrance applications
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Prioritized hub regulator in the GRN with highest betweenness and among highest degree values; directly represses PcPTS and consistently lowers patchouli alcohol accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for editing or silencing to increase patchouli alcohol accumulation, but deployment should consider its positive role in lateral root development and possible stress-related functions
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Primarily nucleus, with minor cytoplasmic localization
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in leaves; lower in roots; expressed across clusters but enriched in root endodermal cells and along epidermal differentiation trajectory
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in leaves; induced by 4 °C, 200 mM NaCl, and 20% PEG6000 treatments
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of target promoter activity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also promotes lateral root formation in transgenic Arabidopsis; may be a membrane-associated latent TF based on minor cytoplasmic signal, but this is speculative
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing may improve patchouli alcohol accumulation but could compromise beneficial root architecture or stress-response functions; overexpression increases lateral roots while reducing target metabolite accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcNAC048 was prioritized from scRNA-seq pseudotime/GRN analysis as a key TF linked to epidermal differentiation and patchouli alcohol pathway genes. 2. PcNAC048 localizes mainly to the nucleus and responds to cold, salt, and drought. 3. Arabidopsis overexpression significantly increases lateral root number without significantly changing primary root length. 4. PcNAC048 directly binds the PcPTS promoter and represses its activity in Y1H and dual-LUC assays. 5. Transient overexpression decreases PcPTS expression and reduces patchouli alcohol by 36.74%, whereas VIGS increases PcPTS expression and raises patchouli alcohol by 59.47%.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lateral root development but represses patchouli alcohol biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PcPTS; predicted HMGR2 (Pat_B04G175400; Pat_B03G170100; Pat_A03G179400); TPS11 (Pat_A03G023200); TPS7 (Pat_A03G034000)
